title: Your 3-Day Istanbul Itinerary
subtitle: The exact plan we'd send a friend. Opinionated. No tourist traps.
intro: This is a 3-day plan that assumes you want to see the big sights without feeling like a tour group. Day 1 is the Old City on foot. Day 2 is the creative side of the Golden Horn. Day 3 crosses to the Asian side because that's where Istanbul stops being a museum and becomes a city. If you only have 2 days, skip Day 3.
preTrip: Get an eSIM before you fly (saves ~$40 vs. airport SIM). Airalo's Turkey plan activates mid-flight.
Pre-book the airport transfer. Istanbul Airport (IST) to Sultanahmet is a 50-min fixed-fare ride via Welcome Pickups; avoid unmarked taxis.
Book the Hagia Sophia combined ticket online. The queue at the door is now brutal.
Download the IETT or Citymapper app. Public transport uses the Istanbulkart — buy and top up on arrival.
Bring US dollars in small bills for one scenario only: the visa-on-arrival kiosk if your passport requires it. Otherwise use card everywhere.

days.title: Day 1 — Sultanahmet (Old City)

flow.what: Breakfast at your hotel. Sultanahmet hotels do the best Turkish breakfast in the city — don't skip it.
Blue Mosque (Sultan Ahmet Camii). Free entry; wear long sleeves. Go first, while it's quiet.
Hagia Sophia. Use your pre-booked ticket. Non-Muslims now enter via the upper gallery.
Lunch at Matbah (Ottoman palace cuisine) or Balıkçı Sabahattin (classic seafood near Cankurtaran).
Basilica Cistern. 20-min visit. Book a time slot — walk-ups wait 45+ min.
Topkapı Palace. 2–3 hours. Pay extra for the Harem — it's the best part.
Walk down to Gülhane Park, loop back via the Arasta Bazaar for rugs and ceramics (cheaper than the Grand Bazaar, same quality).
Sunset drinks at Seven Hills Restaurant rooftop — the Blue Mosque lights up right in front of you. Book a table.
Dinner at Balıkçı Sabahattin or Deraliye Ottoman Cuisine. Walk back to the hotel.

tips: Skip: the Grand Bazaar if you're short on time. It's atmospheric but the sellers are exhausting. The Arasta Bazaar by the Blue Mosque is smaller and friendlier.
Skip: the lira-priced tram stations near the big sights — touts try to sell 'Istanbulkart' for triple price.
If it rains, reverse the day: cistern in the morning, mosques in the afternoon.

days.title: Day 2 — Karaköy, Galata, Beyoğlu
days.staybase: Sultanahmet (morning walk to the tram) or move to a Galata boutique hotel tonight

flow.what: Walk over the Galata Bridge. Fishermen, simit vendors, ferries underneath — the classic Istanbul photo.
Breakfast at Karabatak or Unter in Karaköy. Third-wave coffee scene; this is where Istanbul's creative class starts the day.
Istanbul Modern (now in its new Renzo Piano building) — excellent modern Turkish art, waterfront setting.
Lunch at Çiçek Pasajı (Flower Passage) off Istiklal — old-school meyhane, order meze + raki.
Walk Istiklal Caddesi to Taksim. Stop at Saint Antoine de Padoue (cathedral, free) and the historic tram.
Galata Tower. Skip if the queue is long; the view from the Mikla rooftop bar (see evening) is better.
Coffee and people-watching in Cihangir — the writer/designer neighborhood. Try Kronotrop or Van Kahvaltı Evi.
Cocktails at Mikla (Marmara Pera rooftop) — spectacular panorama. Arrive before sunset.
Dinner. Splurge: Neolokal or Mikla itself. Mid-range: Lokanta Kantin. Casual: Meze by Lemon Tree.

tips: Skip: the people trying to shine your shoes or 'find' a dropped brush on Istiklal. Old scam.
If you're a coffee person: detour to Yeni Lokanta or Mandabatmaz for Turkish coffee done right.
Getting around: T1 tram + F2 funicular + walking handles almost everything.

days.title: Day 3 — Asian side + Bosphorus
days.staybase: Sultanahmet or Beyoğlu — this is a day trip, no move needed

flow.what: Ferry from Eminönü or Karaköy to Kadıköy (20 min, pay with Istanbulkart). Sit on the side facing the Old City for the photo.
Breakfast at Tarihi Moda Börekçisi — legendary börek.
Kadıköy food market (Fish Market area). Try pickles at Sakızcı Hacı Bekir, lokum at Cafer Erol.
Lunch at Çiya Sofrası — chef Musa Dağdeviren's regional Anatolian food. Bucket list stuff.
Walk the Moda seaside promenade. 45-min stroll with tea gardens and the best sunset spot on the Asian side.
Ferry back. For max scenery, take a longer Bosphorus tour ferry: Eminönü → Anadolu Kavağı return (1.5 hrs each way) — way cheaper than private tour boats.
Back to the European side for dinner. Final meal pick: Karaköy Lokantası (classic meyhane) or Mikla for a view.

tips: Kadıköy is where you buy souvenirs that don't feel like souvenirs. Spice mixes, Turkish tea, pottery — all cheaper than Sultanahmet.
The Bosphorus ferry is the single best-value thing you can do in Istanbul. Skip the overpriced private 'dinner cruises'.
Back to the airport: pre-book a return transfer or Havaist bus (90 min, <$5).

scams: The shoeshiner drops his brush, you pick it up, he insists on shining your shoes 'as thanks' then charges €50. Ignore the whole interaction.
Friendly local approaches you, invites you to a 'bar'. You're charged hundreds of euros for a small drink. Never follow a stranger into a bar.
The Grand Bazaar rug 'auction' or 'from my village' pitches — assume 40% of the asked price is the real number.
Taxis taking long routes from the airport. Only use Welcome Pickups, Uber, or BiTaksi (the local app).
Unofficial 'guides' at Hagia Sophia entrance. Real guides have licenses — ask to see.
checklist: One rooftop restaurant reservation (Seven Hills or Mikla)
